_source: MITRE ATT&CK v15, NVD, CISA Advisories
_note: Long-tail attack technique data. Each technique includes detection logic for SOC teams.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
techniques.description: Adversaries abuse PowerShell to execute malicious code. Commonly used for fileless malware, lateral movement, and data exfiltration.
techniques.prevalence: High — used in >60% of enterprise attacks
detection_signals: powershell.exe with -EncodedCommand or -enc flag
PowerShell downloading content: (IEX)(New-Object Net.WebClient)
Base64 encoded payloads in command line
powershell.exe spawned by Office apps (Word, Excel) — critical alert
AMSI bypass attempts: [Ref].Assembly.GetType
techniques.falco_rule:
- rule: Suspicious PowerShell Execution
  condition: spawned_process and proc.name=powershell.exe and proc.args contains "-enc"
  output: PowerShell with encoded args (user=%user.name args=%proc.args)
  priority: WARNING

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Using legitimate credentials to access systems. Often obtained via phishing, credential stuffing, or purchase on dark web.
techniques.prevalence: Very High — #1 initial access vector in 2024-2025
detection_signals: Login from unusual geolocation/IP (impossible travel)
Login outside business hours with privileged account
Successful login after multiple failures
Account accessing resources it never accessed before
Service account used interactively
techniques.falco_rule:
- rule: Privileged Account Unusual Activity
  condition: user.name in (admin_accounts) and not proc.name in (authorized_procs)
  priority: CRITICAL

techniques.name: Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment
techniques.description: Targeted phishing email with malicious attachment. Attacker researches target for personalization (LinkedIn, company website).
detection_signals: Office macros enabled after receiving external email
Email with .lnk or .iso attachment from external sender
Child process of Outlook/Teams: cmd.exe, wscript.exe, mshta.exe
HTML smuggling: script creating blob URL download
mitigations: Block macro execution from internet-sourced files

techniques.name: Process Injection
techniques.description: Injecting malicious code into legitimate processes to evade detection. Code runs under trusted process (lsass.exe, svchost.exe).

techniques.DLL_injection: LoadLibrary called with malicious DLL path
techniques.process_hollowing: Create suspended process, replace memory, resume
techniques.reflective_DLL: DLL loads itself without touching disk (fileless)
techniques.thread_hijacking: Suspend thread, modify instruction pointer, resume

detection_signals: Unexpected network connections from system processes (svchost, lsass)
Memory regions with RWX (Read-Write-Execute) permissions
Cross-process memory writes to critical processes
lsass.exe accessed by non-system processes
techniques.falco_rule:
- rule: LSASS Memory Access
  condition: proc.name != lsass.exe and fd.name contains lsass
  priority: CRITICAL

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact (Ransomware)
techniques.description: Encrypting victim files and demanding ransom. Modern ransomware uses double extortion: encrypt + exfiltrate then threaten to publish.
detection_signals: Mass file rename operations (appending .locked, .enc etc.)
Shadow copy deletion: vssadmin delete shadows
Encryption of file types (.docx, .xlsx, .pdf, .jpg) in rapid succession
README or ransom note creation across multiple directories
Disable Windows Defender: Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring
techniques.falco_rule:
- rule: Ransomware Indicator - Shadow Copy Deletion
  condition: spawned_process and proc.name=vssadmin.exe and proc.args contains delete
  priority: CRITICAL
  output: Shadow copy deletion attempt (user=%user.name)
  tags: [ransomware, critical]

techniques.name: Remote Services: Remote Desktop Protocol
techniques.description: Using RDP to move laterally across network. Often follows valid credential compromise.
detection_signals: RDP connections to non-standard ports (3389 common, but 443/22 used to evade)
RDP from workstation to workstation (should only be server-to-server or admin)
Multiple failed RDP auths followed by success
RDP login with service account or shared admin account
techniques.rogue_rdp: RDP hijacking: attacker takes over existing session without credentials (requires SYSTEM)

techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Systematically attempting passwords. Variants: online brute force, password spray, credential stuffing.

password_spray.definition: One password tried against many accounts (avoids lockout)
password_spray.detection: Multiple failed logins across different accounts from same IP within short window
password_spray.falco_equivalent: SIEM rule: >20 failed logins across 10+ accounts in 10 minutes from same source

credential_stuffing.definition: Using breached username/password pairs from data dumps
credential_stuffing.data_sources: HaveIBeenPwned has 12B+ credentials; dark web markets sell fresh dumps
credential_stuffing.detection: Successful logins from TOR exit nodes or known proxy IPs

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Exploiting vulnerabilities in internet-exposed applications: web servers, VPN, email gateways.

high_value_targets_2024_2025.impact: SQL injection; Cl0p ransomware mass exploitation

detection_signals: Web application scanner signatures in logs
Unusual HTTP methods (OPTIONS, TRACE, PUT to unexpected endpoints)
SQL injection patterns in web logs: UNION SELECT, -- comment, OR 1=1
Path traversal: ../../../../etc/passwd
